codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested modules and native coverage, but two blocking gaps remain: the real Polyglot book is never used in the WASM build, and the public FEN-only API resets repetition history so threefold detection does not work in the documented frontend flow.

issues.description: The __EMSCRIPTEN__ branch always loads the 272-byte embedded fallback (`book_embedded_size` in `engine/src/book_embedded.h:32`) and never attempts to use the bundled `engine/book/rodent.bin`. As a result, the deployed WASM engine does not actually ship the compact 2-5MB opening book requested by the feature; only a tiny fallback book is available at runtime.

`set_position()` reparses the current FEN into a fresh `Position`, and `position_refresh_hashes()` reinitializes history to a single hash (`engine/src/position.c:223-224`). In the documented frontend flow (`set_position(fen)` before each search), this discards all prior positions, so threefold repetition detection only works in native tests that call `movegen_make_move()` directly, not through the public API the website is meant to use.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `engine-worker` skill is out of date for this repo. It tells workers to work in `engine/` and `tests/` and compile `engine/*.c tests/*.c`, but the mission conventions use `engine/src/` and `engine/tests/` with a different gcc command.

The shared library note says repetition detection works without additional frontend state, but the exported API resets history on every `set_position(fen)` call. That note will mislead future workers unless it is corrected or the API is extended to accept/replay move history.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Claim: `.factory/library/engine-book-api.md:5`; documented frontend flow: `.factory/library/architecture.md:95-105`; implementation reset: `engine/src/engine.c:55-66` and `engine/src/position.c:223-224`.

summary: Review failed. The native implementation and tests are substantial, but the feature does not fully meet its runtime intent because the WASM build never uses the real opening book and the public API cannot preserve repetition history across searches.
